notes: DRAFT v0 ROUTE-CONTRACT TEST VECTOR / NON-RUNTIME — Phase 33L. This is a docs-bound route-contract test-vector draft, NOT an executable route test. It does not prove a route exists, does not authorize route implementation, storage writes, auth, or consent, and does not define a production schema.

It maps the Phase 33G route-contract design vector E (ADMISSION-WEDGE-ROUTE-CONTRACT-DESIGN.md §16.E) one layer closer to a possible future route contract; it does NOT replace or mutate the Phase 33E draft v1 probe malformed-or-unsafe-payload-fail-closed.json.

Reason-code alignment (draft): the public reason_code is the stable, existing Dixie-local refusal-family code 'ingress.invalid_request' (grounded in app/src/services/straylight-recall-intake/refusal-mapping.ts), NOT a coined admission-specific public code. A finer private reason family may live on the audit boundary only. The path must fail closed: no transition, no admitted assertion, no public receipt, and no raw payload / unsafe marker / source / stack-trace leak.
Malformed-retry idempotency behavior remains draft (row J). Unresolved Straylight primitive-review rows (E, G, H, K, N, O) are carried forward as markers and are NOT treated as resolved.

unresolved_review_markers.notes: Carried from Phase 33J §5/§6 (review register A-O) and Phase 33K §5/§16. Canonical fail-closed meaning vs Dixie-local refusal reuse (I) is aligned-draft but still requires review; the public reason_code is a real existing Dixie code, not an admission-final code.
